data.title: Blue Owl Capital Corp. Q4 total investment income slips, missing consensus
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE:OBDC) turned in Q4 total investment income that fell short of Wall Street expectations. The business development company had pre-announced Q4 net investment income of $0.47 last month and net asset value per share of $15.26 at Dec. 31, 2024, which slipped from $15.28 at Sept. 30.

Total investment income of $394.4M, lagging the $395.2M consensus, declined from $406.0M in the prior quarter and $411.2M in the year-ago period.

Q4 net investment income per share of $0.47, vs. the average analyst estimate of $0.46, was unchanged from Q3 and fell from $0.51 in Q4 2023.

The decline in NAV per share during the quarter reflected unrealized losses from credit marks in the portfolio, partially offset by over-earning of the base dividend.

Total investments at fair value slipped to $13.2B at the end of Q4 from $13.5B at the end of Q3.

New investment commitments during the quarter totaled $1.7B, offset by $1.6B of sales and repayments, compared with $1.2B of new investment commitments and $1.1B of sales and repayments in the prior quarter.

"Our portfolio has demonstrated resilience and stable credit quality over the past year, and we expect to build on our momentum in 2025 by leveraging the benefits of enhanced scale resulting from our completed merger with OBDE," said CEO Craig W. Packer.

After Q4-end Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE:OBDC) completed its merger with Blue Owl Capital Corp. III (OBDE), with OBDC as the surviving company.

The company's board declared a Q1 regular dividend of $0.37 a share for stockholders of record as of March 31, 2025, payable on or before April 15, 2025. That board also declared a Q4 supplemental dividend of $0.05 per share for stockholders of record as of Feb. 28, 2025, payable on or before March 17, 2025.

Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC) stock fell 0.4% in Wednesday after-hours trading.

Conference call on Feb. 20 at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blue Owl Capital reports Q4 results

data.title: NerdWallet GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.50, revenue of $183.8M beats by $15.46M
data.description:

data.title: Vimeo Holdings GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02, revenue of $103.16M beats by $2.57M
data.description:
• Vimeo Holdings press release (NASDAQ:VMEO): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $103.16M (-2.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.57M.
  • Shares -9.88% AH.
  • 2025 Outlook Demand for video is at an all-time high, and customers want more choices in how it is discovered, delivered, and monetized. Vimeo hosts over 8 billion video minutes and serves over 100 billion views annually. We are now in a strong position to increase investment and pursue accelerating growth profitably. In 2025, we will increase innovation investment in the key areas I mentioned above, utilizing shareholdersʼ resources wisely to accelerate profitable growth in bookings and revenue. We believe that as a result of these investments our growth rates will accelerate as we move through 2025, first in bookings and followed by revenue due to the subscription nature of our business. Vimeo Enterprise and OTT/streaming are delivering meaningful growth and in Q4, together, are now 38% of our bookings and 34% of our revenue. We are seeing positive early impacts of Self-Serve price increases. We have set goals to expand and delight our customer base across even more video use cases and believe if we achieve our goals, we can leave 2025 with double-digit growth in sight. On behalf of the entire Vimeo team, I extend our heartfelt thanks to you, our shareholders, for your belief in our vision for Vimeoʼs future. Customers need Vimeoʼs trust and innovation now more than ever, and we are committed to delivering just that. We are excited about the year ahead and look forward to sharing our progress with you.

data.title: TrueCar targets 20%+ revenue growth in 2025 with expanded TC+ rollout and AI integration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TrueCar, Inc. (NASDAQ:TRUE) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jantoon Reigersman highlighted TrueCar's 2024 performance, with revenue of $175.6M, reflecting a 10.6% year-over-year increase, the strongest growth since 2017. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.6M, an improvement of $15.3M year-over-year. Free cash flow showed a significant improvement, reaching nearly breakeven at (-$0.2M).
  • The CEO emphasized the success of TrueCar+, which allows the purchase and sale of new, used, and certified vehicles online. The platform saw a 50% increase in average monthly purchase initiations and transaction volume in Q4.
  • CFO Oliver Foley noted, "Q4 revenue of $46.2 million, an increase of 11.9% year-over-year, with a positive adjusted EBITDA of $0.4 million." He highlighted the impact of operational efficiency and revenue growth on cash flow improvements.
  • Management stated that the company repurchased 6.1 million shares during 2024 and ended the year with a franchise dealer count of 8,351, a 1.4% year-over-year increase.

Outlook

  • Management expects 2025 revenue to grow by over 20% year-over-year in a normalized new vehicle retail environment, supported by investments in dealer activation, minimizing churn, and expanding OEM partnerships.
  • Modest Q1 2025 revenue growth in the high single digits is anticipated, with a negative adjusted EBITDA of approximately $5M, attributed to increased headcount and marketing expenses.
  • Reacceleration of revenue growth is expected starting Q2 2025, along with fully adjusted EBITDA profitability and breakeven free cash flow.
  • Long-term targets remain at $300M in annual revenue and a 10% free cash flow margin by 2026.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue reached $46.2M, up 11.9% year-over-year. Total unit sales increased by 22% to 93,000 units, with new vehicle sales growing 27.8% year-over-year.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $4.1M, an improvement of $12.2M year-over-year.
  • Franchise dealer revenue showed consistent growth, supported by higher new vehicle sales per dealer, achieving the highest levels since Q3 2021.
  • TrueCar Marketing Solutions (TCMS) contributed to revenue growth, with further development planned for 2025.

Q&A

  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Asked about Q1 guidance and expense increases. CEO Reigersman explained the focus on expanding sales and marketing teams to improve dealer activation and retention. CFO Foley highlighted enhancements in dealer service programs to reduce churn and strengthen relationships.
  • Ryan Powell, B. Riley Securities: Inquired about trends in franchise versus independent dealer churn and the 12-month dealer service program's impact. CEO Reigersman noted the focus on franchise dealers and a lighter service touch for smaller independents.
  • Ryan Meyers, Lake Street: Questioned the Q1 EBITDA guide's impact from wholesale business. CFO Foley clarified that wholesale is not a growth driver and emphasized its role in enabling online transactions rather than affecting gross margins significantly.
  • Chris Pierce, Needham: Asked about dealer lead conversion support. CEO Reigersman detailed efforts to train dealer sales teams, optimize pricing strategies, and enhance lead nurturing processes.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on near-term challenges like Q1 expenses and dealer churn but positively noting management's strategic investments and long-term growth targets.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing growth potential, particularly through TrueCar+ and AI/ML initiatives. However, a slightly defensive tone arose when addressing Q1 guidance and short-term EBITDA impacts.
  • Compared to Q3, the sentiment showed improved confidence, driven by stronger year-end results and progress on strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to include more specific targets for 2025, such as 20%+ revenue growth and breakeven free cash flow by Q2.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include AI-powered fraud detection and deeper dealer management integrations for TrueCar+.
  • Analysts' questions in Q4 emphasized dealer retention and product monetization, reflecting a shift from Q3's focus on revenue and margin improvements.
  • Q4 results showed continued momentum in revenue growth and free cash flow improvements, with expanded progress on TCMS and TrueCar+.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts raised concerns about churn in independent dealers and Q1 revenue growth impacted by headcount investments and the transition of affinity partners like American Express.
  • Management acknowledged these risks but highlighted strategies such as enhanced dealer service programs, marketing efficiency improvements, and expanded affinity partnerships to mitigate impacts.
  • Affordability challenges in the automotive market and high interest rates remain external risks affecting dealer sales and consumer demand.

Final Takeaway

TrueCar ended 2024 with strong financial momentum and significant progress on strategic initiatives like TrueCar+ and AI/ML integrations. While Q1 2025 will see modest growth and higher expenses, management is confident in achieving 20%+ revenue growth for the year, supported by investments in dealer network expansion, enhanced product offerings, and long-term partnerships. The company remains on track to meet its ambitious 2026 targets, with a focus on sustainable growth and innovation in automotive retail.
